behaviors.description: A request whose signature does not match its body is rejected with a 401 response; the body is not forwarded.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is at module handoff (raw-body extraction + signature verifier + enqueue gate), not just pure HMAC math. REAL-INFRA: no real OS/network/subprocess required; in-process HTTP app wiring is enough. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. REJECTION-GATE: rejected System because behavior text only requires 'rejected with 401' and 'not forwarded', which can be validated without real network infrastructure.
behaviors.test_description: Run the handler through an in-process HTTP test harness with real raw-body parsing and signature check wired to a fake queue adapter; send mismatched signature, assert 401 and zero enqueue calls.

behaviors.description: An event is written to the persistent queue before the HTTP 200 acknowledgment is returned to the sender.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this is ordering across modules (handler -> queue persistence -> response). Unit is too low because race/order bugs appear at boundary wiring. REAL-INFRA: does not require real external network or subprocess; local persistent component is sufficient. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. REJECTION-GATE: rejected System because behavior text is ordering of two local actions ('written ... before ... 200'), not OS/network behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Instrument queue write completion and response send in an in-process integration test; assert write commit timestamp/event occurs before response completion.

behaviors.description: The handler returns the HTTP response without waiting for downstream processing of the event to finish.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is async boundary wiring between request path and downstream worker trigger; pure unit logic can miss await/Promise coupling bugs. REAL-INFRA: no real OS/network/subprocess strictly required. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. REJECTION-GATE: rejected System because behavior text is internal non-blocking orchestration, not real network/process dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Use in-process server + intentionally slow downstream processor; verify response completes quickly while processor is still running, then confirm processor finishes later.

behaviors.description: The response arrives within 3 seconds wall-clock time even when downstream processing of earlier events is slow and concurrent.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA: explicit wall-clock SLA under concurrency depends on real runtime scheduling, socket handling, and event-loop behavior; mocks/fake timers can hide contention. MOCK-HIDE: in-process fakes may always pass and miss true latency under concurrent load. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Integration because behavior text explicitly requires 'within 3 seconds wall-clock' under 'slow and concurrent' conditions, which is a real system boundary.
behaviors.test_description: Start the actual service process, generate concurrent load with slow prior events, and measure end-to-end HTTP latency; assert new webhook responses stay under 3 seconds.

behaviors.description: An event whose ID was already received is not written to the queue a second time.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: duplicate suppression is cross-module state handoff (request -> dedupe store/queue write path), so unit-only checks are weak. REAL-INFRA: can be validated with local persistence/in-process components; no real external network/process required. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. REJECTION-GATE: rejected System because behavior text is logical idempotency ('already received ... not written ... second time') and does not require OS/network phenomena.
behaviors.test_description: Send the same event ID twice through the in-process endpoint against a real local persistence-backed queue/dedupe path; assert only one persisted enqueue record exists.

behaviors.description: When the queue reports it is full, the handler returns 503 with a Retry-After header.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: mapping depends on boundary between queue error signaling and HTTP response construction. Unit may miss integration of error type/metadata to status+header. REAL-INFRA: no real OS/network/subprocess required; simulated full signal is enough for this behavior text. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. REJECTION-GATE: rejected System because wording is conditional on 'queue reports it is full' (interface contract), not on physically saturating real infrastructure.
behaviors.test_description: Wire handler with queue module that emits canonical 'full' condition; hit endpoint and assert HTTP 503 plus Retry-After header value/format.

behaviors.description: The persistent queue actually enforces its configured capacity limit and rejects events that would exceed it.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this tests queue internals + persistence interaction under real configured limits; pure mocks can hide enforcement bugs. REAL-INFRA: local DB/filesystem-backed queue is sufficient (Integration definition includes local DB/filesystem); no external API/subprocess required. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. REJECTION-GATE: rejected System because behavior text is component-level capacity enforcement of the persistent queue, not external network/OS-process behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Instantiate the real persistent queue implementation with small capacity, enqueue up to limit, then enqueue one more and assert deterministic rejection/error without corrupting existing entries.
